name: Queen City Jewelry & Pawn
description_short: Queen City Jewelry & Pawn offers collateral-based loans and pre-owned jewelry in Cincinnati with 60+ years of combined jewelry expertise and custom manufacturing services.
description_long:
Queen City Jewelry & Pawn began as a diamond wholesale company over 40 years ago before transitioning to a pawn shop and jewelry store in January 2011 following the 2008-2010 recession. The business is owned and founded by AJ Jhaveri, known as "AJ the Jeweler," and operates under Ohio Division of Financial Institutions regulation and licensing.

The company offers pawn loans on personal items (minimum 120-day hold periods at 6% + $6 per 30 days per Ohio state regulations), buys and sells pre-owned jewelry and watches, sells new items below retail prices, and provides custom jewelry design and manufacturing services. They source inventory from auctions, the internet, and local sellers, featuring luxury watches (Rolex, Audemars Piguet, G-Shock) and general merchandise. Custom jewelry includes detailed renderings, material breakdowns, and 2-3 week turnaround times.

The company distinguishes itself through genuine jewelry expertise (60+ years combined experience and custom manufacturing capability), transparent state-regulated pawn terms clearly documented on their website, competitive pricing described as "well below retail," and a stated commitment to high product standards. They actively market the savings advantage of their pre-owned model and position themselves as Cincinnati's premiere jewelry store with unique sourcing access.

The primary limitation is that this is a pawn shop, not a traditional lender—it requires collateral and functions best for quick cash needs against personal property rather than unsecured credit access. Borrowers must understand Ohio's specific regulatory terms (120-day minimum holds, fixed interest rates, extension-only payment options) and the risk of item forfeiture if loans aren't repaid. Online inventory appears limited to high-end watches; general merchandise availability is not clearly documented.
pros: State-regulated pawn loans with transparent, fixed terms (6% + $6 per 30 days) clearly explained on website
60+ years of combined jewelry expertise and in-house custom manufacturing capability with 2-3 week turnaround
Luxury watch and jewelry inventory including Rolex, Audemars Piguet, and G-Shock models at claimed significant discounts
Unique sourcing from auctions, internet, and local sellers providing items not available in typical retail stores
Custom jewelry design process with detailed renderings and material breakdowns before production
Minimum 120-day loan hold period (longer than many pawn shops) providing extended redemption window
Stated commitment to high product standards with emphasis on quality pre-owned merchandise
cons: Pawn loans require collateral and carry forfeiture risk if not repaid within 120 days—not suitable for unsecured credit needs
Limited transparency on general merchandise inventory online; high-end watch focus may not serve all customer segments
Ohio-specific regulatory terms (fixed 6% + $6/30-day structure) are not flexible and may be less competitive than other pawn shops in different jurisdictions
Custom jewelry process requires upfront design consultation and 2-3 week wait, not ideal for immediate needs
Website does not disclose storage fees, mailing fees, or full redemption schedules for all loan amounts
best_for: Customers needing quick cash against jewelry, watches, or personal items they own
Luxury watch or jewelry buyers seeking below-retail pricing on pre-owned high-end pieces
People wanting custom jewelry design with professional craftsmanship and faster turnaround than typical jewelers
Cincinnati-area residents seeking transparency in regulated pawn lending with clear state-mandated terms
diagnosis: Queen City Jewelry & Pawn is best for customers with jewelry or watches they own who need fast cash against collateral, or for luxury watch/jewelry buyers seeking below-retail pre-owned pieces. The main caveat is that pawn loans carry forfeiture risk if not repaid within 120 days and require surrendering physical items—this is a collateral-based loan model, not unsecured credit, and only works for those with eligible merchandise and redemption capacity.
services: Pawn loans on personal collateral (120-day minimum hold, 6% + $6 per 30 days per Ohio regulation)
Loan redemption and extension services (interest and storage fee payments to extend loan periods)
Buy/sell pre-owned jewelry and luxury watches
Custom jewelry design and manufacturing with 3D renderings
Material sourcing and breakdown for custom pieces
New jewelry and watch retail sales
High-end watch inventory (Rolex, Audemars Piguet, G-Shock models)
Item sourcing from auctions, internet, and local sellers
Jewelry appraisal and valuation services (implied through pawn operations)
Mailing fee services for out-of-area redemptions
